doc.title: Retire bonds at maturity
doc.event: The final coupon has been paid and the term is up. Every dollar of discount or premium has fully amortized, so the carrying value sits exactly at face — and the payoff entry is two clean lines with no gain or loss possible.

variants.label: Pay off at face — no gain, no loss, ever

entries.caption: Maturity date — retire the bonds at face

lines.label: face
lines.why: The liability leaves the books at the amount it was always carried at: face.
lines.trap: If you're reaching for the discount or premium account here, the amortization schedule wasn't finished — at maturity those accounts are already zero.

lines.label: face
lines.why: The contract pays face at maturity. Carrying value = face by construction (the final period plugs to land exactly there), so cash out equals book value and no gain or loss can exist.
lines.trap: A maturity retirement NEVER produces a gain or loss — that's the built-in check on your amortization work.

computationPaths.narration: Amortization exists precisely so the carrying value arrives at face on the last day. Discount climbed up to face; premium slid down to it. Cash out (face) minus carrying value (face) = zero, always.

steps.label: Carrying value at maturity
steps.formulaText: = face (schedule's final row lands here by the plug)

memorize.body: Maturity retirement is the free space on the bingo card: Dr Bonds Payable, Cr Cash, both at face. If an exam shows a gain or loss at MATURITY, something upstream is wrong.
Don't confuse this with EARLY retirement — before maturity the carrying value is NOT face, and the difference against the reacquisition price is a real gain or loss.

questions.prompt: Bonds issued at a discount are held to maturity and paid off. What gain or loss is recognized at retirement?

distractors.feedback: That's the ORIGINAL discount — but it fully amortized over the term. At maturity carrying value equals face, so the payoff produces exactly zero gain or loss.

traces.label: Final schedule row ↔ face ↔ the two-line payoff

build.scaffold: Two lines, both at face. Every other account in the bank is a decoy — including the discount, which finished amortizing on the last coupon date.
